description: record overwrite aboveVariation (ADR 01073): the span always records to staging; at stopRecord a span verdict decides whether the staged capture replaces the existing recording (and baselines update with it) or is discarded. Baselines persist between tests/reruns and tests aren't contractually ordered, so every assert accepts either clean outcome — refreshed (changed true) or kept (changed false) — while NaN-guarding against the verdict not running. In practice the two identical-span tests exercise both branches in one job: whichever runs first seeds (changed true), the other compares clean against the just-written baselines (changed false). Permutations: implied checkpoints (bare aboveVariation) and an explicit checkpoints object tuning maxVariation. The aboveVariation + checkpoints:false combo is overridden to checkpoints-on (unit-tested in test/recording-above-variation.test.js); dirty-span and promote-failure paths are unit-level too.

tests.description: Bare aboveVariation: checkpoints implied with defaults.

steps.description: Assert the verdict ran (changed is a boolean), no checkpoint errors, and the recording file exists at the reported absolute path in both outcomes.
steps.runShell: node -e "const changed = process.env.AV_CHANGED; const ok = (changed === 'true' || changed === 'false') && Number(process.env.AV_ERRORS) === 0 && require('fs').existsSync(process.env.AV_PATH); process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1)"

tests.description: The identical span again: against the sibling test's baselines this compares clean (changed false); on a fresh tree whichever test runs first seeds instead. Either way the verdict must have run cleanly.

steps.description: Assert the verdict ran cleanly and the recording exists.
steps.runShell: node -e "const changed = process.env.AV2_CHANGED; const ok = (changed === 'true' || changed === 'false') && Number(process.env.AV2_ERRORS) === 0 && require('fs').existsSync(process.env.AV2_PATH); process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1)"

tests.description: aboveVariation with an explicit checkpoints object: custom maxVariation and baseline directory still drive the verdict.

steps.description: Assert the tuned verdict ran cleanly and the recording exists.
steps.runShell: node -e "const changed = process.env.AVT_CHANGED; const ok = (changed === 'true' || changed === 'false') && Number(process.env.AVT_ERRORS) === 0 && require('fs').existsSync(process.env.AVT_PATH); process.exit(ok ? 0 : 1)"
